case.title: U.S. POSTAL SERVICE v. LETTER CARRIERS

opinion.h4: U.S. POSTAL SERVICE v. LETTER CARRIERS, (1988)

UNITED STATES POSTAL SERVICE v. NATIONAL ASSOCIATION OF LETTER CARRIERS, AFL-CIO CERTIORARI TO THE UNITED STATES COURT OF APPEALS FOR THE DISTRICT OF COLUMBIA CIRCUIT No. 87-59. Argued April 20, 1988 Decided April 27, 1988
opinion.p: Certiorari dismissed. Reported below: 258 U.S. App. D.C. 260, 810 F.2d 1239.

Glen D. Nager argued the cause for petitioner. With him on the briefs were Solicitor General Fried, Assistant Attorney General Willard, Deputy Solicitor General Wallace, William Kanter, and Marc Richman.
Keith E. Secular argued the cause and filed a brief for respondent.*

f.p: Briefs of amici curiae urging affirmance were filed for the American Federation of Labor and Congress of Industrial Organizations by David M. Silberman and Laurence Gold; and for the National Council on Compulsive Gambling, Inc., by Albert B. Lewis.
The writ of certiorari is dismissed as improvidently granted.
